Apple___Apple_scab.description: Apple scab is a fungal disease that causes olive-green to black scabs on leaves and fruit, leading to premature leaf drop.
organic_treatment: Rake and destroy fallen leaves in autumn to reduce winter spores.
Apply neem oil or sulfur-based organic sprays preventatively.
chemical_treatment: Apply fungicides containing myclobutanil or captan early in the season.

Apple___Black_rot.description: Black rot is a fungal disease causing brown leaf spots, fruit rot, and cankers on branches.
organic_treatment: Prune out dead or diseased wood during the dormant season.
Remove mummified fruit from the tree and ground.
chemical_treatment: Apply mancozeb or captan-based fungicides starting at petal fall.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.description: A fungal disease that requires both apple and cedar trees to complete its life cycle, causing bright orange spots on leaves.
organic_treatment: Remove nearby eastern red cedar trees or prune out the galls in winter.
Apply copper fungicides early in the spring.
chemical_treatment: Use protective fungicide sprays like myclobutanil as soon as blossoms open.

organic_treatment: Maintain a regular watering schedule.
Prune annually to maintain good airflow.
Maintain soil acidity (pH 4.5 to 5.5).
Apply pine needle or bark mulch to retain moisture.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.description: Powdery mildew appears as a white, powdery fungal growth on leaves, distorting new growth.
organic_treatment: Apply sulfur dust or potassium bicarbonate sprays.
Ensure good air circulation through proper pruning.
chemical_treatment: Apply systemic fungicides like tebuconazole if the infection is severe.

organic_treatment: Monitor for aphids and maintain good soil drainage.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.description: A fungal disease causing rectangular, pale brown-to-gray lesions on corn leaves, severely reducing yields.
organic_treatment: Practice crop rotation with non-host crops.
Plow under infected crop debris after harvest.
chemical_treatment: Apply foliar fungicides containing pyraclostrobin or azoxystrobin before tasseling.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.description: Common rust causes elongated, brick-red pustules on both surfaces of corn leaves.
organic_treatment: Plant rust-resistant corn hybrids.
Ensure adequate spacing to reduce humidity in the canopy.
chemical_treatment: Apply a protectant fungicide early if rust appears before the silking stage.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.description: Characterized by long, cigar-shaped, grayish-green to tan lesions. It thrives in moderate temperatures with heavy dew.
organic_treatment: Rotate crops away from corn for at least one year.
Till soil deeply to bury infected debris.
chemical_treatment: Apply systemic fungicides like propiconazole if lesions spread to the upper leaves.

organic_treatment: Ensure consistent watering, especially during the silking and tasseling phases.

Grape___Black_rot.description: A devastating fungal disease that turns grape berries into black, shriveled mummies.
organic_treatment: Remove all mummified fruit from vines and the ground during winter.
Apply copper or sulfur sprays strictly as a preventative.
chemical_treatment: Apply mancozeb or myclobutanil fungicides starting from early shoot growth.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).description: A complex fungal trunk disease causing 'tiger-stripe' patterns on leaves and dark spotting on berries.
organic_treatment: Prune vines late in the dormant season to minimize spore infection.
Remove and burn deeply infected cordons or trunks.
chemical_treatment: No highly effective chemical cures exist; focus on preventative pruning wound protectants.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.description: A minor fungal disease causing irregular brown spots with dark margins on the lower leaves.
organic_treatment: Rake and destroy fallen leaves to remove the overwintering fungus.
Improve canopy airflow via summer pruning.
chemical_treatment: Standard broad-spectrum fungicides used for other grape diseases will usually control this.

organic_treatment: Keep the canopy well-managed for airflow and sun exposure.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).description: A fatal bacterial disease spread by the Asian citrus psyllid, causing mottled leaves and bitter, misshapen green fruit.
organic_treatment: Infected trees cannot be cured and MUST be removed to prevent spreading.
Use organic insecticidal soaps to control the psyllid population on healthy trees.
chemical_treatment: Apply systemic insecticides like imidacloprid to control the psyllid vector.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.description: A bacterial disease causing small, dark, angular lesions on leaves that eventually drop out, leaving 'shot holes'.
organic_treatment: Maintain optimal tree nutrition to increase natural resistance.
Avoid overhead irrigation.
chemical_treatment: Apply copper-based bactericides or oxytetracycline during the growing season.

organic_treatment: Apply dormant oil sprays in winter to prevent spring pest outbreaks.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.description: Bacterial spot causes water-soaked lesions on leaves and fruit of pepper plants, thriving in warm, humid weather.
organic_treatment: Apply organic copper sprays cautiously.
Avoid working with or touching the plants when they are wet.
chemical_treatment: Apply bactericides containing streptomycin (watch for local resistance).

Pepper,_bell___healthy.description: The bell pepper plant appears healthy.
organic_treatment: Maintain consistent soil moisture to prevent blossom end rot.

Potato___Early_blight.description: Early blight is a fungal disease that causes 'bullseye' patterned brown spots on older, lower leaves.
organic_treatment: Apply a biofungicide containing Bacillus subtilis.
Rotate crops and remove all plant debris after harvest.
chemical_treatment: Use broad-spectrum fungicides like chlorothalonil or mancozeb.

Potato___Late_blight.description: A highly destructive water mold disease that causes large, dark lesions on leaves and massive tuber rot. (The cause of the Irish Potato Famine).
organic_treatment: Destroy infected plants immediately.
Plant certified disease-free seed potatoes.
chemical_treatment: Apply systemic fungicides containing mefenoxam or cymoxanil immediately upon detection.

organic_treatment: Hill up soil around the base of the plant to protect developing tubers from sun exposure.
Prune out old, woody canes to encourage new growth.
Monitor for aphids and ensure good soil drainage.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.description: A common fungal disease creating a white, dusty coating on squash leaves, stunting plant growth.
organic_treatment: Spray with neem oil or a baking soda/liquid soap mixture.
Water at the base of the plant to keep leaves dry.
chemical_treatment: Apply fungicides with the active ingredient chlorothalonil.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.description: A fungal disease causing solid purple to brown spots on leaves, lacking the light center seen in other leaf spots.
organic_treatment: Remove and destroy heavily infected leaves.
Plant in well-draining soil and avoid dense, overcrowded patches.
chemical_treatment: Apply protective fungicides like captan during wet spring weather.

organic_treatment: Use straw mulch to keep berries off the bare soil and prevent rot.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.description: Causes small, water-soaked, greasy spots on leaves and raised, scabby spots on tomato fruits.
organic_treatment: Remove heavily infected plants to save nearby healthy ones.
Use a copper-based organic spray to slow the spread.
chemical_treatment: Apply a mixture of mancozeb and copper hydroxide.

Tomato___Early_blight.description: A common fungal disease characterized by small, dark lesions with concentric rings (bullseyes) on lower leaves.
organic_treatment: Remove infected lower leaves immediately.
Apply compost tea or copper-based fungicides.
chemical_treatment: Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil or azoxystrobin.

Tomato___Late_blight.description: Late blight is a fast-spreading, destructive disease causing large, dark, greasy blotches on leaves and white mold on the undersides.
organic_treatment: Remove and bag affected plants immediately. Do NOT compost them.
Ensure very good air circulation around remaining plants.
chemical_treatment: Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil aggressively if detected early.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.description: A fungal disease primarily affecting greenhouse or high-humidity tomatoes, causing pale green spots on upper leaves and olive-green mold underneath.
organic_treatment: Reduce humidity significantly by increasing ventilation and pruning lower leaves.
Avoid overhead watering.
chemical_treatment: Apply fungicides containing difenoconazole or cyprodinil.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.description: A destructive fungal disease causing numerous small, circular spots with dark borders and gray centers, leading to rapid defoliation.
organic_treatment: Remove infected leaves immediately and sanitize tools.
Mulch deeply to prevent soil spores from splashing onto lower leaves.
chemical_treatment: Apply chlorothalonil-based fungicides on a 7-10 day schedule.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.description: Tiny arachnids that suck sap from leaves, causing a stippled, yellowed appearance and fine webbing on the undersides.
organic_treatment: Spray undersides of leaves with a strong blast of water to dislodge them.
Apply insecticidal soap or neem oil.
chemical_treatment: Use targeted miticides like abamectin or bifenazate (broad-spectrum insecticides often worsen spider mite outbreaks).

Tomato___Target_Spot.description: A fungal disease causing dark brown lesions with concentric rings on leaves and sunken spots on the fruit.
organic_treatment: Improve air flow through aggressive pruning.
Apply copper-based organic fungicides.
chemical_treatment: Apply fungicides like mancozeb or chlorothalonil.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.description: A severe viral disease transmitted by whiteflies. Plants are stunted, and leaves curl upward and turn yellow at the edges.
organic_treatment: Pull and destroy infected plants immediately—the virus cannot be cured.
Use reflective mulches and sticky traps to deter whiteflies.
chemical_treatment: Apply insecticides like imidacloprid to control the whitefly vectors, not the virus itself.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.description: A highly contagious virus causing mottled light and dark green patterns on leaves and stunted growth. It is easily spread by hands and tools.
organic_treatment: Destroy infected plants immediately by burning or bagging.
Thoroughly wash hands and sanitize tools with bleach before touching healthy plants.
chemical_treatment: No chemical treatments exist for plant viruses.

Tomato___healthy.description: The tomato plant appears to be healthy.
organic_treatment: Monitor regularly for signs of pests or disease.
Prune suckers to maintain good airflow and redirect energy to fruit production.
